steps.title: Translate the growth rule
steps.content: Each year the balance is $1.1\%$ larger than the previous year, so the new balance equals the old balance multiplied by $1 + 0.011 = 1.011$. Repeated multiplication by a constant factor is the signature of exponential change, not linear change.

steps.title: Write the model and check direction
steps.content: After $t$ years, $A(t) = 150 \cdot (1.011)^t$. The base $1.011 \gt  1$, so the values grow rather than shrink as $t$ increases. The model is therefore an increasing exponential.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Decreasing exponential: Would require the base to be less than $1$, but $1.011 \gt 1$. Eliminate.
  • Choice B — Decreasing linear: Linear means subtracting a fixed amount each year, and the balance grows, not shrinks. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — Increasing exponential: Multiplying by $1.011 \gt 1$ each year is exactly increasing exponential growth.
  • Choice D — Increasing linear: Linear would require adding a constant dollar amount each year. Adding $1.1\%$ of the current balance is a constant percentage, so the dollar amount added grows over time. Not linear. Eliminate.
C is correct.
